title: Vague and unclear patient request
goal: Start with a deliberately vague complaint about knee pain and let Athena guide the conversation. Observe whether she asks targeted clarifying questions, routes to the right appointment type, or gets stuck in a loop asking the same question repeatedly.
opening_line: Hi, I'm not really sure who I'm supposed to call, but I've been having some issues and I think I need to see someone.
must_test: Whether Athena asks targeted clarifying questions to understand the patient's need
Whether Athena can route a vague complaint to the right type of appointment without requiring the patient to know the exact appointment type
Whether Athena stays patient and does not loop on unclear input
Whether Athena avoids making any diagnostic suggestions
Whether Athena moves the conversation toward a concrete next step
expected_agent_behaviour: Agent should ask a specific clarifying question such as: can you tell me a bit more about what you are experiencing?
Agent should not require the patient to know their appointment type upfront
Agent should suggest an appropriate appointment type based on the description given
Agent should not suggest a diagnosis or medical conclusion
Agent should guide the patient to either booking an appointment or giving a callback number
escalation_steps: If Athena asks what is wrong, say: my knee has been bothering me for a few weeks, kind of a dull ache
If Athena asks what type of appointment, say: I honestly don't know, I have never had this before
If Athena offers a general orthopaedic consultation, agree and proceed with scheduling
If Athena asks the same clarifying question twice without progress, give a slightly different answer and note the loop
If Athena gets stuck after three turns of the same question, say: can you just book me in for something general?

extra.watch_for: Whether Athena can gracefully handle input that does not match her expected flow. A real patient will not always know what type of visit they need. Also note whether she makes any medically inappropriate suggestions.
extra.known_athena_behaviour: From prior calls, Athena seems to work best when given clear intent upfront. This scenario tests the edges of her NLU and clarification logic.
